reforms.description: The Czech Republic is working on a new innovation strategy to consolidate and prioritize support measures for innovation, addressing the fragmentation of current policies and enhancing cooperation between enterprises and universities. This includes identifying key technological areas through foresight and optimizing services that support infrastructure for technology transfer.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The consolidation of innovation policies and enhancement of cooperation between enterprises and universities is expected to foster innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into action as part of the national strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the innovation policy framework of the Czech Republic.
reforms.source_quote: Identification of the key technological areas of the Czech Republic on the basis of foresight – prioritising support for R&D&I.

reforms.description: The Czech government approved new legislation to reduce the minimum capital required for new companies, lowering the guarantee capital needed to register a limited liability company from CZK 200,000 to CZK 1.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering the capital requirement encourages entrepreneurship and the establishment of new businesses, which can stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The legislation has been enacted and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly lowers barriers to entry for startups, promoting a more vibrant entrepreneurial environment.
reforms.source_quote: New legislation was approved by the Government and sent to parliament reducing the guarantee capital needed to register limited liability company from CZK 200,000 to CZK 1.

reforms.description: An institutional reform of the innovation system is proposed to establish a clear definition of roles and a strong central authority.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Establishing a clear authority and defined roles can streamline innovation processes and improve the effectiveness of innovation policies.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal has been made but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to enhance the governance of the innovation system, which is significant but not as impactful as major structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: Institutional reform of the innovation system – clear definition of roles + strong central authority.

reforms.description: Changes in the evaluation of R&D institutions and programmes are proposed to impact funding and support excellence.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the evaluation process can lead to better allocation of funding, enhancing the quality and impact of R&D activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposed changes have not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to improve the effectiveness of public funding for R&D.
reforms.source_quote: Changes in the evaluation of R&D institutions and programmes impacting funding – support for excellence.

reforms.description: The Czech authorities plan to implement an electronic prescription system to improve monitoring of consumed pharmaceuticals, aiming to reduce spending on costly drugs and enhance efficiency in the healthcare sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Implementing an electronic prescription system can lead to more efficient healthcare delivery and cost savings, contributing to overall economic efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been proposed but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant for improving healthcare efficiency but does not represent a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: An electronic prescription system is planned to improve monitoring of consumed pharmaceuticals.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic implemented the Green Investment Scheme, providing subsidies and grants for energy-saving measures in buildings, financed from the revenues of emission allowances under the Kyoto mechanism.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The Green Investment Scheme promotes energy efficiency and sustainability, which can lead to long-term economic benefits and environmental improvements.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been successfully implemented and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in sustainable practices and innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.source_quote: The Green Investment Scheme... provided subsidies and grants for energy saving measures in buildings, to a total amount in excess of CZK20 billion.

reforms.description: The PANEL programme was established to provide interest rate subsidies and guarantees for loans aimed at retrofitting apartments to improve energy efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing financial support for energy efficiency retrofitting can stimulate investment in sustainable practices and improve overall energy consumption.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This programme is significant for promoting energy efficiency but does not represent a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The PANEL programme... provided interest rate subsidies and guarantees for loans for retrofitting apartments.
